summary:
Palpable purpura is characteristic of Henoch-Schönlein purpuric disease, mixed cryoglobulinaemia, and drug-induced vasculitis—all involving small-vessel inflammation. In contrast, giant cell arteritis primarily affects medium-to-large vessels and lacks typical palpatible purpura; it instead causes systemic symptoms like headaches or vision changes.  

Final Answer: C

Note: The summary above focuses only on clinically significant facts related directly to the presence/absence of palpable purpura among listed conditions.
